title: How did an Afghan woman journalist’s writing resonate in China?
content:
A story of cross-border empathy between Chinese readers and Afghan women

Originally published on Global Voices

Letters from an Afghan woman. Image used with permission.

When Afghan journalist Khadija Haidary fled the Taliban, she never imagined that her writing would reach readers thousands of miles away in China. Yet it did — prompting small but meaningful acts of support that empowered her to move forward amid her uncertain situation. In China, where civil society is tightly regulated and spontaneous cross-border humanitarian support is rare, her letters, which evolved into a book titled “

title: Cameroonian rice farmers caught between national ambition and rural realities
content:
Rice accounts for more than half of Cameroon’s foreign grain supplies

Originally published on Global Voices

Farmers harvesting rice in a rice field (paddy) in Cameroon. Screenshot from the video “How to grow rice in Cameroon?” on the My Agric Pro YouTube channel

In January 2023, the Cameroonian government announced a policy of rice self-sufficiency, the population’s staple food. In the country’s northwest region, rice farmers combine courage with determination to fulfil these national promises.

title: African women make economic gains through global gig economy
content:
Women make up about 27 percent of the Africa’s online gig workforce

Originally published on Global Voices

A smartphone displaying a ride-hailing app. Photo by Adesewa Olofinko. Used with permission.

At 9:00 a.m. in Lagos, Nigeria, when traffic starts easing from its daily rush-hour gridlock, Vivian opens her laptop. 

Her commute is just twelve steps from her bedroom to the adjoining room that doubles as her office. Inside, a large desk anchors the space, paired with an ergonomic chair, a gaming computer, a wide monitor, and two compact laptops.
